basic_details.job_title: Punjab & Sind Bank Cloud & AI Engineer Recruitment 2026 - 8 Contractual Posts, Apply by 28 June
basic_details.short_information: Punjab & Sind Bank, a Government of India Undertaking, has released a notification inviting applications for 8 contractual posts across its Cloud Centre of Excellence and Artificial Intelligence verticals. The posts span three grade levels - MMGS II, MMGS III, and SMGS IV. Online applications open on 12 June 2026 and close on 28 June 2026. Experienced IT professionals in cloud engineering and AI/ML development should apply at the bank's official website, https://punjabandsind.bank.in/.

application_fee.details: The application fee is non-refundable. Payment must be made online only - cash, cheques, money orders, and postal stamps are not accepted. Bank transaction charges for the online payment gateway are borne by the candidate. | Once submitted, an application cannot be withdrawn, and the fee will not be refunded under any circumstances, nor held in reserve for any other examination.

qualification.details: All eligibility conditions are assessed as on the respective cut-off dates stated above. Candidates must independently verify they meet every criterion before applying; merely submitting an application does not constitute a right to be called for selection. | The maximum ages above apply to General category and EWS candidates. SC/ST candidates receive 5 years of relaxation in the upper age limit; OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates receive 3 years; Persons with Benchmark Disabilities receive 10 years; Ex-Servicemen (with at least 5 years' military service) receive 5 years; and persons affected by the 1984 riots receive 5 years. SC/ST/OBC candidates may combine their category relaxation with only one of the remaining relaxation categories. | Post 1 - Manager, Cloud Centre of Excellence (MMGS II) Mandatory qualification: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Science & Engineering, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or Electronics & Communications Engineering (or equivalent) from a university/institute recognized by the Government of India, UGC, or AICTE. Preferred certifications include AWS Solutions Architect (Associate/Professional), Azure Administrator, DevOps Engineer, or Google Associate/Professional Cloud Engineer. | Work experience required: Minimum 4 years of post-qualification experience in IT Industry, BFSI, or the IT vertical of an organization, of which at least 2 years must be specifically in cloud administration or cloud engineering roles. | Post 2 - Manager, AI Engineers (MMGS II) Mandatory qualification: Full-time B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electronics, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, or Machine Learning - OR - full-time MCA/M.Tech in AI, Machine Learning, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field - from a university/institution recognized by the Government of India, AICTE, or UGC. Candidates must also hold at least one professional certification (valid as on the date of eligibility) in AI, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Data Science, or Generative AI from a recognized university, reputed technology organization, or professional certification body. | Work experience: Minimum 6 years (post B.E./B.Tech/MCA) in AI/ML development, Data Science, or Advanced Analytics, including at least 2 years leading or managing AI/ML projects or teams - OR - Minimum 5 years (post M.Tech) with the same specialization, including the same 2-year leadership requirement. | Post 3 - Senior Manager, Cloud Centre of Excellence (MMGS III) Same mandatory degree requirement as Post 1. Preferred certifications: AWS Solutions Architect (Professional), Azure Administrator, DevOps Engineer, or Google Associate/Professional Cloud Engineer. | Work experience: Minimum 6 years post-qualification experience in IT/BFSI, of which at least 3 years in cloud architecture or cloud engineering roles. | Post 4 - Senior Manager, AI Engineers (MMGS III) Same mandatory degree and certification requirements as Post 2 (Manager AI Engineers). | Work experience: Minimum 4 years (post B.E./B.Tech/MCA) in AI/ML development, Data Science, or Advanced Analytics - OR - Minimum 3 years (post M.Tech) in the same domains. | Post 5 - Chief Manager, Cloud Centre of Excellence (SMGS IV) Same mandatory degree as Posts 1 and 3, plus a mandatory intermediate certification from Microsoft Azure. Preferred additional qualifications: MCA or M.Tech/M.Sc in Computer Science, IT, or Electronics & Communications Engineering. Preferred certifications: AWS Solutions Architect (Professional), Azure Solutions Architect Expert, or Google Professional Cloud Architect. | Work experience: Minimum 8 years post-qualification experience in IT/BFSI, of which at least 4 years in cloud architecture, cloud engineering, or DevOps roles. | Candidates should note that 59.99% will be treated as less than 60%, and 54.99% as less than 55%, when calculating percentage of marks. Where a university awards CGPA/OGPA, candidates must convert it to percentage and indicate that figure in the online application.

selection_process: The bank will use a multi-stage selection process for all posts. All three scales - SMGS IV
and MMGS II - follow the same procedure: Written Test → Short-listing → Personal Interview.
The minimum qualifying score in each section is 40% for General and EWS candidates and 35% for Reserved Category candidates. However

only Professional Knowledge marks are used for drawing the merit/rank list and shortlisting candidates for the Personal Interview; marks in English Language and General Awareness are not included in the final merit calculation.

Candidates clearing the written test are called for a Personal Interview. The minimum qualifying score for the interview is 40% for Unreserved and EWS candidates and 35% for Reserved categories. Final selection is based on the combined marks from the written test and Personal Interview
in order of merit ranking. Where two candidates tie at the cut-off
they are ranked in descending order of age.
The Personal Interview will be conducted in New Delhi unless otherwise specified. Call letters will be sent by email only. Candidates must attend at their own cost.
The bank shortlists candidates based on experience and suitability at its own discretion - meeting the minimum qualification does not guarantee an interview call.
The bank has designated examination centres across India. Candidates may opt for a centre in their state
subject to availability. The bank reserves the right to allot a different centre or cancel/add centres. No request for a change of examination centre
or session will be entertained. Selected centres include: Vishakhapatnam (Andhra Pradesh)
how_to_apply: Applications are accepted online only through the bank's official website. The steps below reflect the application flow described in the notification.
Visit https://punjabandsind.bank.in/ and navigate to the Recruitment section on the home page.
Click on the relevant notification for Cloud CoE / AI Engineers.

Before starting registration, scan and prepare the following files: Passport-size photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 20–50 KB; JPG/JPEG/PNG) Signature on white paper in black ink (10–20 KB) Left thumb impression on white paper (20–50 KB; JPG/JPEG) Hand-written declaration in English, on white paper in black ink - text: "I, [Name], hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required." (50–100 KB; JPG/JPEG; NOT in capital letters) Supporting documents in PDF format: Brief Resume, ID Proof, Age Proof, Educational Certificates, Experience Certificates/NOC, Caste Certificate (if applicable)
Passport-size photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 20–50 KB; JPG/JPEG/PNG)
Signature on white paper in black ink (10–20 KB)
Left thumb impression on white paper (20–50 KB; JPG/JPEG)

Hand-written declaration in English, on white paper in black ink - text: "I, [Name], hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required." (50–100 KB; JPG/JPEG; NOT in capital letters)
Supporting documents in PDF format: Brief Resume, ID Proof, Age Proof, Educational Certificates, Experience Certificates/NOC, Caste Certificate (if applicable)
Complete the online registration form. Keep the registered email ID and mobile number active throughout the process.
Upload all documents at the specified fields. The system will not proceed until photographs and signatures meet specifications.
Pay the application fee online. Transaction charges are borne by the candidate.
Submit the application and save/print the system-generated application form with the registration number.
Apply separately for each post if applying for more than one position.
In case of multiple submissions for the same post, only the last valid completed application will be retained; fees paid for earlier submissions will be forfeited.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Cloud & AI Engineer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application, make any edits, and pay the application fee is 28 June 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in this Punjab & Sind Bank recruitment?
faq.answer: A total of 8 posts are available — 4 Manager (Cloud CoE), 1 Senior Manager (Cloud CoE), 1 Chief Manager (Cloud CoE), 1 Manager (AI Engineers), and 1 Senior Manager (AI Engineers).

faq.question: Q3. Is this a permanent or contractual job?
faq.answer: This is a contractual engagement for an initial period of three years, subject to annual performance review. It may be extended for up to two additional years at the bank's discretion. It is not a permanent or regular appointment.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for Punjab & Sind Bank Cloud & AI Engineer 2026?
faq.answer: General, EWS, and OBC candidates pay ₹850 plus applicable taxes and payment gateway charges. SC, ST, and PwBD candidates pay ₹100 plus applicable taxes and payment gateway charges. The fee is non-refundable.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for these posts?
faq.answer: All posts follow a three-stage process: Written Test → Short-listing → Personal Interview. The written test has 100 questions worth 100 marks in 105 minutes, covering English Language, General Awareness, and Professional Knowledge. Only Professional Knowledge marks are used for shortlisting candidates for interview.

faq.question: Q6. What is the minimum CIBIL score required?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a minimum CIBIL score of 650 at the time of joining.

faq.question: Q7. What educational qualification is needed for the AI Engineer posts?
faq.answer: A full-time B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science, IT, Electronics, Data Science, AI, or Machine Learning — or a full-time MCA/M.Tech in a related field — from an institution recognized by the Government of India, AICTE, or UGC. Additionally, candidates must hold at least one valid professional certification in AI, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Data Science, or Generative AI.

faq.question: Q8. Where is the personal interview conducted?
faq.answer: The personal interview will be conducted at New Delhi unless otherwise specified. Call letters are sent by email only. Candidates must attend at their own expense.

faq.question: Q9. How do I apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Cloud CoE / AI Engineer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Visit https://punjabandsind.bank.in/, go to the Recruitment section, and click the relevant notification. Complete the online registration, upload all required documents, pay the fee online, and submit. A candidate must apply separately for each post.

faq.question: Q10. What is the age limit for the MMGS II posts?
faq.answer: For MMGS II (Manager-level posts), the minimum age is 29 years and the maximum is 41 years as on 01 June 2026. Candidates must have been born between 02 June 1985 and 01 June 1997 (both dates inclusive). Relaxation applies as per government guidelines for reserved categories.
